classification_rules.included: The source claim or story beat is represented in the VN, usually compressed or dramatized.
classification_rules.partial: Only part of the selected passage is represented, or the VN materially simplifies its qualifications.
classification_rules.excluded: No evidence mapping is asserted. In the viewer this remains unhighlighted.
classification_rules.fabricated: VN-only connective tissue, characters, dialogue, or staging. These annotations are not source highlights.

mappings.beat: Two workforces; scale and economics of AI agents
Labs seek to automate AI R&D while pulling up the ladder
Congressional attention, control question, and 2027 Transparency Act
mappings.note: The VN preserves the policy arc but replaces examples and analysis with a hearing and character drama.

mappings.beat: AI dominates the 2028 election and makes the intelligence explosion politically salient
Five-way 2029 policy choice
Plan D: light-touch regulation and a race through automated AI R&D
Plan D risks: loss of control, concentrated power, and war
mappings.note: The VN converts the source's argumentative assessment into a short catastrophic ending.

mappings.beat: Plan C: brief pause, rushed safety plan, and restart under China pressure
Plan C risks: weak safety assurance, oligarchy, and exclusion of the wider world
Plan B coalition, Project consolidation, sabotage, and handoff-versus-war dilemma
Plan B critique: secrecy, race pressure, power concentration, and escalation
Plan A announcement, Chinese incentives, verifiability, and temporary training halt
Compute declaration and international inspection
Temporary training pause and inference-only verification
mappings.note: Device alternatives and implementation caveats are omitted.

mappings.beat: Worldwide buy-in and formation of the Consortium
Plan A's four principles
Buy time without freezing forever
Open algorithms and broad evaluation access, but closed weights
Transparency changes incentives and makes AI more competitive
Diffuse frontier capability among many companies and countries
Reversibility and Mutually Assured Compute Destruction
Near-superhuman systems, safety incidents, and safety cases built on alignment plus control
Public dispute over continual learning and negotiated global restrictions
Alternate failure: a flawed safety case is approved
mappings.note: The source only links to a branch here; the VN stages a complete interactive branch.

mappings.beat: 60 million fast agents, physical bottlenecks, and the industrial explosion
Fast growth creates covert-project risk and monitored industrial zones
2032 Citizen's Dividend and international distribution excluding China
mappings.note: The source baseline supports the permit-funded dividend, $45K US payment, $1.2K international payment, and China exclusion. The named corporation and exact 25% mechanism do not appear in source-main.md.

mappings.beat: Using new wealth for biosecurity and limiting mass persuasion
Compute expands from millions to tens of billions of chips; 200 million fast agents
Floating ocean energy and datacenters
Pause when AI reaches top-expert level
Alignment research becomes more hopeful but control remains necessary
Post-work economy divided into industrial zones, arcologies, and preserves
Meaning and status after employment
AI-assisted truth-seeking exposes secrets and destabilizes politics
Alignment becomes a scientific field with huge incentives
Growing trust and debate over empowering AIs
2040 handoff and the emotionally fraught arrival of superintelligence
Space governance, universal rights, and self-replicating probes
Plural post-scarcity futures and continued human choice
Plan S opening: seek a global moratorium rather than build god
Plan S verification, economic adaptation, and fragility of an indefinite halt
Cap-and-trade permits replace the collapsing wage-tax base
Alternate covert-project threat and inspection response
mappings.note: source-main.md contains only the alternate-timeline summary/link here. The VN's exact anomaly, player options, and outcomes are new interactive staging.

mappings.beat: Hidden compute and defection remain hazards during a slowdown
Alternate deal-dissolution turning point
mappings.note: The local source baseline only announces and links the alternate timeline. The VN makes a specific election crisis playable.

mappings.beat: Deal collapse risk and the compute-destruction guarantee
By 2035, the US dividend reaches about $1M and international payments about $10K
mappings.note: The chronological later-year placement is faithful to the source amounts. The exact 75% corporation share is not present in source-main.md.

mappings.beat: Public-POV bonus compresses the lived transition from AI coworker to post-work abundance
mappings.note: The underlying milestones are source-grounded; the anonymous public viewpoint and summary format are VN-original.

mappings.beat: Insider-POV bonus compresses automated R&D, transparency, and safety-case culture
mappings.note: The underlying research arc is source-grounded; the anonymous lab-worker viewpoint and summary format are VN-original.

fabricated_annotations.label: Player viewpoint
fabricated_annotations.note: The senior-adviser protagonist and second-person framing are VN inventions used to connect policy events.

fabricated_annotations.label: Reed hearing
fabricated_annotations.note: Marcus Reed, Meridian AI, and the hearing dialogue are fictional dramatization.

fabricated_annotations.label: Lux demonstration
fabricated_annotations.note: Lux is a VN-original recurring AI persona; her product demonstration is not in the source passage.

fabricated_annotations.label: Park's failed-tests incident
fabricated_annotations.note: Lena Park and the concrete deleted-tests anecdote are fictional, though they dramatize source concerns about deception and alignment.

fabricated_annotations.label: Election-room cast
fabricated_annotations.note: The President, Chen, Park, Reed, and Lux dialogue is original connective drama around the source election beat.

fabricated_annotations.label: Catastrophic montage
fabricated_annotations.note: The source points to AI 2027 and gives an argument; the VN invents a compact narrated endpoint and imagery.

fabricated_annotations.label: Rushed-safety ending
fabricated_annotations.note: Specific meetings and final imagery are VN dramatization of the branch assessment.

fabricated_annotations.label: War-room ending
fabricated_annotations.note: Dialogue and staging are invented; strategic claims track the source.

fabricated_annotations.label: Private decision scene
fabricated_annotations.note: The Oval Office decision and named-character reactions are VN-original framing.

fabricated_annotations.label: Consortium confrontation
fabricated_annotations.note: Reed's dialogue personifies institutional positions not assigned to this fictional character by the source.

fabricated_annotations.label: Interactive safety-case vote
fabricated_annotations.note: The player choice, exact buried assumption, and failure sequence are an interactive VN construction from a short alternate-timeline pointer.

fabricated_annotations.label: Dividend presentation and mechanism
fabricated_annotations.note: The chronology and payment amounts track source-main.md. The Compute Dividend Corporation name, Alaska analogy, and exact 25% share are not present in that local baseline; staging and connective prose are adaptation material.

fabricated_annotations.label: Park and Lux ceiling scene
fabricated_annotations.note: The parent-and-child analogy and personal dialogue are VN-original dramatization.

fabricated_annotations.label: Final meeting and countdown
fabricated_annotations.note: Character farewells and the protagonist's final meeting are original; the handoff and countdown derive from source beats.

fabricated_annotations.label: Lux-guided epilogue
fabricated_annotations.note: Lux's narration and intimate closing frame are invented around selected source space-governance ideas.

fabricated_annotations.label: Playable covert-project turning point
fabricated_annotations.note: Covert defection is source-grounded, but the ambiguous power anomaly, binary player choice, US counter-project, inspection resolution, and explicit ending are VN-created mechanics.

fabricated_annotations.label: Playable deal-breakdown turning point
fabricated_annotations.note: Deal dissolution and compute destruction are source-grounded. The election trigger, border-choice interface, and exact success/failure sequences are VN-created mechanics.

fabricated_annotations.label: Public and Insider POV summaries
fabricated_annotations.note: These unlockable viewpoints, anonymous narrators, and retrospective-summary format are VN-original. Their milestones are cross-referenced separately where source-grounded.
